objective: Phase 3 #5 — exercise debug.smartPress across multiple native macOS apps to collect real data on AX-action coverage vs pointer-click fallback. Each case picks a (target app, query) tuple; the runtime decides per invocation whether AX wins or the recipe falls back to a real pointer click. Strategy outcome is recorded in signals (smartPress.strategy / smartPress.fallbackUsed); the recipe does not lock either outcome.
preconditions: The target app declared by app_id is installed and reachable.
Screen Recording and Accessibility permissions are already granted.

The desktop is in a quiescent state: no human is typing, clicking, or moving the cursor during the run. Any contamination invalidates the evidence (see recipes/macos/demo/dual-cursor-press-notes.cases.v0.json for the case where this rule was originally written down).

notes: Budget is pointer because debug.smartPress is allowed to fall back to a real pointer click when AX fails. When the AX path wins, the actual run will record cursorDisturbance=none; when the fallback fires it will record cursorDisturbance=warp. The recipe deliberately does not assert which one happened.
Discovery recipe — every case starts as candidate, and the matrix exists to gather data on how often the AX path wins across (app, query) tuples.

Promotion to validated requires multiple hands-off replays on a quiescent desktop, not a single successful smoke. A successful one-off invocation while a human is collaborating with the AX target (e.g. switching to the right window, dismissing modals) is contaminated evidence and must not promote any case.

steps.purpose: Press the target control via debug.smartPress. Whether the AX path or the pointer-click fallback runs is recorded in signals.smartPress.strategy.

expected_signals: debug.smartPress completes without error (either AX path or pointer fallback succeeded).
An evidence display capture is produced after the press.
The smartPress driver response records signals.smartPress.strategy and signals.smartPress.fallbackUsed so downstream analysis can tell which path ran.
success_criteria: The smart-press step produced at least one artifact (the smartPress driver always writes one).
The capture-evidence step produced at least one artifact.
non_goals: This recipe does not lock smartPress.strategy. A run that took the AX path is just as valid as a run that fell back to pointer; both produce data.

This recipe does not validate that the pressed control actually triggered its intended effect (e.g. Notes did not necessarily create a new note even on a clean smartPress run). Verifying the post-press state is the job of the app-specific recipe (macos.notes.create_and_verify_note.v2), not this discovery matrix.
This recipe does not assert cursorDisturbance — it varies by strategy.

known_limits.ax_tree_drift: Whether the smartPress query resolves to an AX node depends on the live UI state at observation time. The same query can succeed on one replay and fail on another if window focus, modal stack, or scroll position changed.
known_limits.single_run_is_not_validation: Phase 3 #5 explicitly requires that no case is promoted to validated based on a single successful run; this is the lesson recorded in macos.demo.dual_cursor_press_notes.cases.v0.json after run_1779371986165_16879_0.
known_limits.shared_app_state: Do not run multiple cases against the same live app instance concurrently.
